title: dalea enneandra var. pumila
intro: Dalea enneandra var. pumila, also known as dwarf prairie rose or Mexican dalea, is a perennial plant species belonging to the pea family (Fabaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of isoflavones and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Dalea enneandra var. pumila include its potential to alleviate menopausal symptoms, reduce inflammation, and support bone health, among others. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
